articles.title: Stock Futures Mixed as Iran Attack on Israel Shakes Fragile Ceasefire
articles.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel on June 7, shattering the April ceasefire. Israel retaliated with airstrikes. S&P 500 sank 2.64% to 7,383.74 on Friday, Nasdaq dropped 4.18% — worst week since April 2025. South Korea Kospi crashed 8.4%, Nikkei -3.85%. Oil surged: Brent $96.05 (+3.18%), WTI $93.67 (+3.46%). Nearly $2 trillion erased from US markets Friday after strong jobs report (172K vs 80K expected) fueled rate-hike fears. Trump says US close to final Iran deal, warned Israel against retaliation.

articles.title: Crude Oil Prices Surge 4% After Iran Missile Strikes on Israel; Asian Markets Tumble Up To 8%
articles.summary: Brent above $96, WTI above $93. Dollar index held around 100. Kospi fell 8% to 7,442 (recovered to 7,568). Nikkei slumped 3% (~3,000 pts). US nearly $2T erased Friday. Magnificent Seven all closed red. Broadcom -7%. Trump claims US 'on verge of final agreement with Iran,' plans to call Netanyahu urging restraint. First direct Iranian attack on Israel since April ceasefire.

articles.title: Brent Crude at $97.39 — Up 4.62%, +45% YoY; Strait of Hormuz Near-Closure
articles.summary: Brent at $97.39 (+4.62% daily, +45.27% YoY). OPEC+ approved +188K bpd July production increase despite supply risks. China crude imports at 10-year low. Strait of Hormuz remains near-closed. 12-month forecast: $108.15. Note: monthly change still -6.55% — oil had been falling before this escalation.

articles.title: May Jobs Report: 172K Payrolls — More Than Double Expectations; Rate Cut Hopes Fade
articles.summary: 172K jobs added vs 80K expected. Unemployment 4.3% unchanged. Wage growth +0.3% MoM/+3.4% YoY. March+April revisions added +93K. Leisure/hospitality +70K (World Cup boost). Treasury yields jumped sharply higher. Rate cuts off near-term horizon per analysts. Q1 GDP 1.6% annualized; Atlanta Fed tracking ~3% Q2. Fed focused on persistent inflation, not labor weakness.

articles.title: Fed Meeting Tracker: Next FOMC June 16-17; Polymarket 99% 'No Change'
articles.summary: Current rate: 3.50-3.75%. Next FOMC June 16-17 (SEP included). March SEP: PCE 2.7% for 2026, GDP 2.4%, unemployment 4.4%. CPI jumped 2.4%→3.3% in March. Rate HIKE sentiment rising — 'for first time in years, prevailing sentiment is a rate hike much more likely than cut.' Polymarket: 99% no change at June meeting, 1% for 25bp cut. Powell: Iran war, tariffs pushing energy costs higher → feeds inflation.

articles.title: SpaceX Targets Record $75B IPO at $135/Share — $1.75T Valuation, June 12 Debut
articles.summary: Fixed price $135/share (no range). 555.6M shares, all primary. $75B raise — largest IPO ever (3x Alibaba). Ticker SPCX on Nasdaq, June 12. Musk retains 82% voting control. 2025 rev $18.67B, net loss $4.94B. Only Starlink profitable. P/S at 93.7x. 30% retail allocation. Roadshow started June 5. Morningstar fair value $780B — 48% below IPO price. xAI merger valued at $1.25T combined in Feb. Anthropic and OpenAI IPOs also expected.

articles.title: Strategy: Saylor Revives Bitcoin-Buy Speculation; Executives Plan $15M Stock Sales
articles.summary: Saylor posted signature BTC acquisition chart Sunday, hinting at new purchase. CEO Phong Le: 'Our corporate strategy is to increase net Bitcoin.' But: Strategy sold 32 BTC for $2.5M in late May (first net disposal since 2022). Two execs plan $15M share sales (CEO $11.1M, CFO $3.9M). Bitcoin below $60K — lowest since Oct 2024. June 8 shareholder vote on dividend timing. Scrutiny growing over finances and dividend obligations.

articles.title: Buy, Sell or Hold MSTR After Strategy's First Bitcoin Sale in Years?
articles.summary: MSTR at $126, down 72% from July 2025 highs, -31% in last month. Q4 2025: $17.44B unrealized loss. Polymarket: 63% MSCI delisting probability by end 2026. Holding 843K BTC at $75,699 avg cost; current BTC ~$64K. Sale was tiny (0.0038% of holdings) but broke psychological contract. Verdict: HOLD. Next 1-2 weeks critical — BTC purchase announcement would restore confidence; another sale would signal structural shift.

articles.title: Semiconductor Selloff: Broadcom -12%, Marvell -17%; Broadcom Preferred at 64x vs 90x
articles.summary: MRVL Q1 FY2027: rev $2.4B (+28% YoY), guided +35% current quarter, ~40% FY growth. Huang called it 'next trillion-dollar company.' AVGO Q2 FY2026: rev $22.2B (+48%), AI semi $10.8B (+143%), FY AI guidance >$100B. Both hit hard on AI spending concern + macro. AVGO at 64x earnings, MRVL at 90x. Analysis favors AVGO: faster growth, cheaper, stronger FCF ($10.3B/quarter). Customer concentration risk for both.

articles.title: Super Micro Closes $2B AI Infrastructure Deal with Gorilla Technology in India
articles.summary: SMCI closed $2B AI infrastructure deal with Gorilla Technology (GRRR) for India. Covers 20,736 B300 cards, 5,120 B200 cards, networking equipment. SMCI up 34.5% YTD, ~20% last month, but -9.7% past week amid semis selloff. Helios AI factory platform launch. Analyst price targets $15-50.

articles.title: Rivian R2 SUV Order Phase Begins June 9 — 8-Day Rally Ahead of Mass-Market Push
articles.summary: RIVN surged for 8 straight days ahead of R2 SUV order phase starting June 9. R2 is Rivian's mass-market competitor to Tesla Model Y. 2026 delivery guidance 62K-67K vehicles (up from 42K in 2025). Stock trading ~$16-18 range. Key catalyst: order book strength and pricing revealed at June 9 kickoff.

articles.title: IG Credit: AI Capex Boom Driving Record $2.25T Supply in 2026 — Spreads at 25-Year Tights
articles.summary: IG spreads at levels last seen mid-1990s. AI capex driving unprecedented supply: $600B in 2026, $4T cumulative through 2030. Meta, Oracle, Google, Amazon raised >$100B combined in Q4 2025 alone. 2026 supply forecast $2.25T (+35% YoY). For first time since 2022, supply may outpace demand. Banks look compelling; technology warrants caution. Alpha from what you DON'T own. Selective, defensive positioning recommended.

articles.title: Gold Falls to $4,294 — Down 9.32% Monthly Despite Iran-Israel Escalation
articles.summary: Gold at $4,294.21, down 0.85% daily, down 9.32% monthly, still +29.12% YoY. Record high was $5,181+ in January 2026. Safe-haven bid has faded despite renewed Iran-Israel conflict. Rising rate expectations and stronger dollar pressuring gold. Contrarian signal: geopolitical crisis NOT driving gold higher. J.P. Morgan forecast $5,000/oz by Q4 2026, $6,000 possible longer term.

articles.title: Defense Stocks Hit Pause as Trump Pentagon Remakes Military Industrial Complex
articles.summary: Defense stocks (RTX, LMT, NOC, GD) seeing mixed signals. Trump Pentagon remaking procurement priorities toward advanced warfare, missile defense, space-based systems. NATO allies increasing defense budgets. KeyBanc initiated coverage favoring 'new space' over legacy primes: RKLB, ASTS highlighted. Iran conflict should benefit defense but Trump's restructuring introduces uncertainty.

articles.title: DXY Rises to 100.14 — Up 2.23% Monthly on Strong US Data, Rate Differential
articles.summary: DXY at 100.14, +0.07% daily, +2.23% monthly, +1.22% YoY. Dollar supported by robust US labor market and rate differential. 2Y yield rose ~10bps to 4.16%. Strong dollar headwind for multinational earnings and EM flows. Rate hike narrative strengthening dollar further vs EUR, JPY.
